pdg_node.statement: (s0: float — initial mid-price of the asset, inventory: float — starting inventory position (signed)) -> tuple[float — absolute risk-aversion coefficient, float — market-depth / order-arrival rate parameter, float — current inventory position, float — current mid-price, float — asset price volatility]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Bootstraps the market-maker's immutable parameter state from a supplied initial mid-price and inventory position, materialising the five scalar fields — risk-aversion (gamma), market-depth (k), inventory (q), mid-price (s), and volatility (sigma) — that all downstream computations consume as pure inputs.

declaration.name: marketmakerstateinit
declaration.type_signature: (s0: float — initial mid-price of the asset, inventory: float — starting inventory position (signed)) -> tuple[float — absolute risk-aversion coefficient, float — market-depth / order-arrival rate parameter, float — current inventory position, float — current mid-price, float — asset price volatility]
declaration.docstring: Bootstraps the market-maker's immutable parameter state from a supplied initial mid-price and inventory position, materialising the five scalar fields — risk-aversion (gamma), market-depth (k), inventory (q), mid-price (s), and volatility (sigma) — that all downstream computations consume as pure inputs.
declaration.conceptual_summary: ImmutableParameterSpaceInitializer. Takes a minimal set of exogenous scalar seed values — one representing the current state of a measurable quantity and one representing a signed positional offset — and expands them into a fully-specified, frozen parameter manifold of five scalars that collectively define the operating envelope for all downstream computations. The transform acts as a pure injection: it maps a low-dimensional initialization tuple into a higher-dimensional configuration space, materializing both the supplied runtime values (current magnitude, signed position) and the hardcoded structural constants (sensitivity coefficient, arrival-rate parameter, dispersion coefficient) as a single immutable record. No computation is performed beyond assignment; the output is a static snapshot that serves as the canonical ground truth for all subsequent stateful operations.. Applications: Epidemiological modeling: initializing an SIR/SEIR compartmental model with a seed infection count (signed-position analog) and a baseline population contact rate (magnitude analog), materializing transmission rate (beta), recovery rate (gamma), initial susceptible population (S0), and variance of stochastic contact events — consumed by all downstream differential equation solvers, Robotics / motion planning: bootstrapping a PID or LQR controller's parameter record from an initial sensor reading (measured state magnitude) and a signed positional error offset, locking in the gain coefficients (proportional, derivative, integral), process-noise covariance, and reference setpoint as an immutable configuration consumed by every control-loop iteration, Quantum circuit simulation: constructing an immutable Hamiltonian parameter record from an initial observable expectation value and a signed spin-polarization offset, materializing coupling strength, decoherence rate, initial state amplitude, and energy dispersion constants that all gate-operation kernels reference as pure inputs, Structural engineering / finite element analysis: seeding an FEA node's material-property record from an initial stress magnitude and a signed strain offset, fixing Young's modulus (stiffness coefficient), Poisson's ratio (lateral-coupling parameter), current displacement vector, reference load magnitude, and material yield dispersion — forming the immutable per-element parameter block consumed by the global stiffness-matrix assembler

pdg_node.statement: (gamma: float — absolute risk-aversion coefficient, k: float — market-depth / order-arrival rate parameter, q: float — current inventory position, s: float — current mid-price, sigma: float — asset price volatility) -> tuple[float — optimal bid quote, float — optimal ask quote, float — inventory-adjusted mid-price, float — total bid-ask spread]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Applies the Avellaneda-Stoikov closed-form formulae to derive (1) the inventory-skewed reservation price r = s − q·γ·σ² and (2) the symmetric optimal half-spread δ = γ·σ²·T + (2/γ)·ln(1 + γ/k), yielding a firm bid quote (r − δ/2) and ask quote (r + δ/2) that maximise expected utility under inventory risk.

declaration.name: optimalquotecalculation
declaration.type_signature: (gamma: float — absolute risk-aversion coefficient, k: float — market-depth / order-arrival rate parameter, q: float — current inventory position, s: float — current mid-price, sigma: float — asset price volatility) -> tuple[float — optimal bid quote, float — optimal ask quote, float — inventory-adjusted mid-price, float — total bid-ask spread]
declaration.docstring: Applies the Avellaneda-Stoikov closed-form formulae to derive (1) the inventory-skewed reservation price r = s − q·γ·σ² and (2) the symmetric optimal half-spread δ = γ·σ²·T + (2/γ)·ln(1 + γ/k), yielding a firm bid quote (r − δ/2) and ask quote (r + δ/2) that maximise expected utility under inventory risk.
declaration.conceptual_summary: RiskAversion-Weighted Symmetric Interval Placement with Positional Skew. Given a scalar reference point and a signed displacement (positional imbalance), this transform first skews the reference point proportionally to the displacement, a quadratic variance measure, and a risk-aversion scalar — producing a bias-corrected central anchor. It then computes a symmetric half-interval around that anchor whose width is jointly determined by the risk-aversion scalar, the variance measure, a time-horizon scalar, and a logarithmic correction term derived from an arrival-rate parameter — yielding a lower bound and upper bound that are equidistant from the corrected anchor and guaranteed to straddle it.. Applications: Adaptive sensor threshold placement in signal detection systems: given a sensor's baseline signal level, its noise variance, and a signed accumulation of recent false-positive or false-negative errors (positional imbalance), compute skew-corrected upper and lower detection thresholds that minimize expected classification loss under a specified penalty-per-deviation parameter., Robotic joint torque bracketing in motion control: given a robot actuator's nominal torque setpoint, the variance of disturbance forces, and the current signed deviation of the joint from its neutral pose, derive an asymmetry-corrected torque command with symmetric upper/lower saturation bounds that balance trajectory tracking against mechanical stress risk., Dynamic pricing interval generation in resource allocation systems: given a baseline resource valuation scalar, the variance of demand fluctuations, and a signed measure of current resource surplus or deficit, compute a corrected target allocation rate and symmetric upper/lower offer bounds that maximize expected throughput utility under inventory-imbalance risk., Ecological habitat corridor width optimization: given a baseline migration corridor centerline, the variance of species dispersal distances, and a signed measure of current population density asymmetry across the corridor, derive a skew-adjusted centerline and symmetric lateral boundary offsets that maximize expected gene-flow utility while penalizing large positional deviations from carrying-capacity balance.
